query: India Rajya Sabha elections 2026 unopposed contested results
answer:
# India Rajya Sabha Elections 2026: Unopposed & Contested Results

## Overview

The 2026 Rajya Sabha biennial elections are being held to elect **74 members** (including 2 casual vacancies) of India's 245-member upper house. The elections are spread across three phases: **March, June, and November 2026**.

---

## March 2026 Election (16 March 2026) — COMPLETED

### Key Headline: 26 of 37 Seats Decided Unopposed

**37 Rajya Sabha seats** across 10 states were up for election. Of these, **26 seats (70%) were decided unopposed**, with actual voting occurring only in **3 states** covering **11 seats**.

### States Where Candidates Were Elected UNOPPOSED (26 seats)

| State | Seats | Key Winners |
|---|---|---|
| **Maharashtra** | 7 | Vinod Tawde (BJP), Sharad Pawar (NCP-SP), Parth Pawar (NCP-Ajit), Ramdas Athawale (RPI-A) |
| **Tamil Nadu** | 6 | Tiruchi Siva (DMK), Anbumani Ramadoss (PMK), M. Thambidurai (AIADMK) |
| **West Bengal** | 5 | Babul Supriyo (TMC), Koel Mallick (TMC), Menaka Guruswamy (TMC), Rajeev Kumar (TMC), Rahul Sinha (BJP) |
| **Assam** | 3 | Jogen Mohan (BJP), Terash Gowalla (BJP), Pramod Boro (UPPL) |
| **Chhattisgarh** | 2 | Laxmi Verma (BJP), Phulo Devi Netam (INC) |
| **Telangana** | 2 | Abhishek Manu Singhvi (INC), Vem Narender Reddy (INC) |
| **Himachal Pradesh** | 1 | Anurag Sharma (INC) |

### States Where Elections Were CONTESTED (11 seats)

| State | Seats | Key Winners |
|---|---|---|
| **Bihar** | 5 | Nitish Kumar (JD-U), Ram Nath Thakur (JD-U), Nitin Nabin (BJP), Shivesh Kumar (BJP), Upendra Kushwaha (RLM) |
| **Odisha** | 4 | Manmohan Samal (BJP), Sujeet Kumar (BJP), Santrupt Misra (BJD), Dilip Ray (Independent) |
| **Haryana** | 2 | Sanjay Bhatia (BJP), Karamvir Singh Boudh (INC) |

---

## Party-Wise Seat Tally (March 2026 — 37 seats)

| Party | Seats Won |
|---|---|
| **BJP** | 12 |
| **INC (Congress)** | 5 |
| **TMC** | 4 |
| **JD(U)** | 2 |
| **DMK** | 2 |
| **NCP (Ajit Pawar)** | 1 |
| **NCP-SP (Sharad Pawar)** | 1 |
| **Shiv Sena** | 1 |
| **RPI(A)** | 1 |
| **AIADMK** | 1 |
| **PMK** | 1 |
| **DMDK** | 1 |
| **UPPL** | 1 |
| **RLM** | 1 |
| **BJD** | 1 |
| **Independent** | 1 |
| **Nominated** | 1 |

### Alliance-Wise Breakdown (March)
- **NDA:** ~21 seats (BJP 12, JD(U) 2, Shiv Sena 1, NCP-Ajit 1, RPI(A) 1, UPPL 1, RLM 1, AIADMK 1, PMK 1)
- **INDIA bloc:** ~13 seats (INC 5, TMC 4, DMK 2, NCP-SP 1, DMDK 1)
- **Others:** 3 seats (BJD 1, Independent 1, Nominated 1)

---

## June 2026 Election (18 June 2026) — UPCOMING

The June round covers ~24 seats across 10+ states. Key states include:

| State | Seats | Expected Outcome |
|---|---|---|
| Andhra Pradesh | 4 | TDP 2, JSP 1, BJP 1 (NDA sweep expected) |
| Gujarat | 4 | BJP expected to win all 4 |
| Karnataka | 4 | BJP 1, INC 3 |
| Madhya Pradesh | 3 | BJP 2, INC 1 |
| Rajasthan | 3 | BJP 2, INC 1 |
| Jharkhand | 2 | INDIA alliance expected |
| Others (Arunachal, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram) | 1 each | Regional/NDA parties |

Nominations close **8 June 2026**; polling on **18 June 2026**.

## November 2026 Election — FUTURE

| State | Seats |
|---|---|
| Uttar Pradesh | 10 |
| Uttarakhand | 1 |

UP is the most significant contest: NDA (294 MLAs) can win ~7 seats; INDIA bloc (104 MLAs) can win ~2 seats. The 10th seat's outcome depends on smaller parties and pending by-elections.

---

## Notable Candidates & Highlights

1. **Nitish Kumar** — Former Bihar CM, elected to Rajya Sabha from Bihar (contested)
2. **Sharad Pawar** — Veteran leader re-elected unopposed from Maharashtra
3. **Parth Pawar** — Sharad Pawar's grandnephew, elected from rival NCP faction
4. **Koel Mallick** — Bengali film actress, elected from West Bengal (TMC)
5. **Menaka Guruswamy** — Noted Supreme Court advocate, elected from West Bengal (TMC)
6. **Rajeev Kumar** — Former Kolkata Police Commissioner, elected from West Bengal (TMC)
7. **Harivansh Narayan Singh** — Former RS Deputy Chairman, nominated by the President

---

key_facts: The March 2026 Rajya Sabha elections covered 37 seats across 10 states, held on 16 March 2026.
26 of 37 seats (70%) were decided unopposed; only 11 seats in 3 states (Bihar, Haryana, Odisha) went to actual voting.
BJP won the most seats (12) in the March round, followed by INC (5) and TMC (4).
NDA alliance won approximately 21 of 37 March seats; INDIA bloc won approximately 13.
Former Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar was elected to the Rajya Sabha from Bihar in a contested election.
Sharad Pawar (NCP-SP) was re-elected unopposed from Maharashtra; his grandnephew Parth Pawar was elected from the rival NCP (Ajit Pawar) faction.

Notable first-time Rajya Sabha entrants include actress Koel Mallick (TMC), Supreme Court advocate Menaka Guruswamy (TMC), and former Kolkata Police Commissioner Rajeev Kumar (TMC) — all from West Bengal.
The June 2026 round (18 June) covers approximately 24 seats across 10+ states; nominations close 8 June 2026.
The November 2026 round covers 11 seats — 10 in Uttar Pradesh and 1 in Uttarakhand.
Pre-election NDA strength in the Rajya Sabha was approximately 108 elected + 5 nominated (~132 total with allies); INDIA bloc had approximately 65 seats.
Unopposed states in March: Maharashtra (7), Tamil Nadu (6), West Bengal (5), Assam (3), Chhattisgarh (2), Telangana (2), Himachal Pradesh (1).
Contested states in March: Bihar (5 seats), Odisha (4 seats), Haryana (2 seats).
